1.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Malik al-Ash'ari (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The greatest betrayal before Allah on the Day of Resurrection is a cubit of land that lies between two men or between two partners in a house. They divide it, then one of them steals a cubit of land from his companion, and so he will be made to wear it as a collar from seven earths.'
1.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Malik al-Asy'ari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Pengkhianatan terbesar di sisi Allah pada Hari Kiamat adalah sehasta tanah yang berada di antara dua orang atau dua orang yang berserikat dalam sebuah rumah. Mereka membaginya, lalu salah seorang mencuri sehasta tanah dari milik temannya, maka ia akan dikalungkan kepadanya dari tujuh lapis bumi.'

2.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Malik al-Ash'ari (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Shall I not tell you about the prayer of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ?' He then described how the Prophet ﷺ gave the salam to his right and to his left, then said: 'This is the prayer of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.'
2.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Malik al-Asy'ari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Maukah kalian aku ceritakan tentang shalat Rasulullah ﷺ?' Lalu ia menggambarkan bagaimana Nabi ﷺ mengucapkan salam ke kanan dan ke kiri, kemudian berkata: 'Inilah shalat Rasulullah ﷺ.'

3.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Buhayna (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ stood up in the second rak'ah of Dhuhr prayer and forgot to sit, and when he had finished his prayer just before the taslim, he performed two prostrations, then concluded with the taslim.
3.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Buhaynah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berdiri pada rakaat kedua shalat Dhuhur dan lupa duduk, dan ketika beliau telah selesai dari shalatnya menjelang salam, beliau sujud dua kali kemudian mengakhiri dengan salam.

4.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Buhayna (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ led us in a prayer — we think it was 'Asr — and he stood in the second rak'ah without sitting. When he was about to give the taslim, he performed two prostrations.
4.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Buhaynah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ mengimami kami dalam shalat — kami mengira itu adalah Ashar — dan beliau berdiri pada rakaat kedua tanpa duduk. Ketika akan mengucapkan salam, beliau sujud dua kali.

5.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Malik ibn Buhayna (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ saw a man praying the two rak'ahs of Fajr sunnah after the iqamah had been called. When the prayer was finished, the people gathered around him, and the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Are you praying Fajr as four rak'ahs?'
5.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah bin Malik Ibnu Buhaynah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ melihat seorang laki-laki shalat dua rakaat sunnah Fajar setelah iqamat dikumandangkan. Ketika shalat selesai, orang-orang mengerumuni orang itu, dan Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Apakah kamu shalat Subuh empat rakaat?'

6.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Buhayna (may Allah be pleased with him), who was a companion of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Did any of you recite with me just now?' They said: 'Yes.' He said: 'I was thinking to myself: why am I being contested in the Quran?' So the people refrained from reciting behind him when he said that.
6.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah Ibnu Buhaynah (radhiyallahu 'anhu), yang merupakan sahabat Rasulullah ﷺ, bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Apakah ada di antara kalian yang membaca bersamaku tadi?' Mereka menjawab: 'Ya.' Beliau bersabda: 'Aku berkata kepada diriku: mengapa aku dipersaingkan dalam bacaan Al-Qur'an?' Maka orang-orang berhenti membaca di belakangnya ketika beliau mengucapkan hal itu.

7.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Buhayna (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, when he prostrated, would spread his arms out so that the whiteness of his armpits could be seen.
7.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Buhaynah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ ketika sujud, beliau merenggangkan kedua tangannya sehingga tampak putihnya ketiak beliau.

8.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Buhayna (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ had himself cupped at Luhyi Jamal on the road to Makkah, on the middle of his head, while he was in the state of ihram.
8.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah bin Buhaynah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berbekam di Lahyi Jamal di jalan menuju Makkah, di bagian tengah kepala beliau, sementara beliau dalam keadaan ihram.

9.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Malik ibn Buhayna (may Allah be pleased with him) that when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ prayed, he would spread his arms apart so that the whiteness of his armpits could be seen.
9.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah bin Malik Ibnu Buhaynah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ ketika shalat, beliau merenggangkan kedua tangannya sehingga tampak putihnya ketiak beliau.

10.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Malik ibn Buhayna (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ passed by a man who was praying the two rak'ahs before Fajr after the iqamah had been called. The Prophet ﷺ said something to him that we could not hear. When we gathered around him asking what the Messenger of Allah ﷺ had said, he told us: 'He said to me: One of you will soon be praying Fajr as four rak'ahs.'
10.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah bin Malik Ibnu Buhaynah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melewati seorang laki-laki yang sedang shalat dua rakaat sebelum Fajar setelah iqamat dikumandangkan. Nabi ﷺ mengatakan sesuatu kepadanya yang tidak kami dengar. Ketika kami mengerumuni orang itu dan bertanya apa yang dikatakan Rasulullah ﷺ, ia memberitahu kami: 'Beliau berkata kepadaku: Hampir saja salah seorang dari kalian shalat Subuh empat rakaat.'

11.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Malik ibn Buhayna (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ passed by him while he was prolonging his prayer — or something similar — before the Fajr prayer. The Prophet ﷺ said to him: 'Do not make these two rak'ahs like the sunnah of Dhuhr before and after it — put a distinction between them.'
11.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah bin Malik Ibnu Buhaynah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ melewatinya ketika ia sedang memperpanjang shalatnya — atau semisalnya — sebelum shalat Fajar. Nabi ﷺ bersabda kepadanya: 'Janganlah kalian jadikan dua rakaat ini seperti sunnah Dhuhur sebelum dan sesudahnya — buatlah pemisah di antara keduanya.'

12.iwh_en: It was narrated from Malik ibn Buhayna (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man entered the mosque after the iqamah had been called and prayed the two rak'ahs of the Fajr sunnah. When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ had finished the congregational prayer, the people gathered around that man, and the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Are you praying Fajr as four rak'ahs?'
12.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Malik Ibnu Buhaynah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki masuk masjid setelah iqamat dikumandangkan lalu shalat dua rakaat sunnah Fajar. Ketika Rasulullah ﷺ selesai shalat berjamaah, orang-orang mengerumuli laki-laki itu, dan Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Apakah kamu shalat Subuh empat rakaat?'

13.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Buhayna (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ led us in two rak'ahs then stood up without sitting. The people stood with him. When he completed his prayer and we were awaiting his taslim, he said the takbir and performed two prostrations while sitting before the taslim, then gave the salam.'
13.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah Ibnu Buhaynah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ mengimami kami dua rakaat kemudian berdiri tanpa duduk. Orang-orang pun berdiri bersamanya. Ketika beliau menyelesaikan shalatnya dan kami menantikan salamnya, beliau bertakbir lalu sujud dua kali dalam keadaan duduk sebelum salam, kemudian salam.'

14.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Buhayna (may Allah be pleased with him), who was among the companions of the Prophet ﷺ, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ led them in two rak'ahs, then stood without sitting. The people stood with him. When he had completed the last two rak'ahs, the people waited for his taslim; he then said the takbir, prostrated, then said the takbir again, prostrated, then gave the salam.
14.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah Ibnu Buhaynah (radhiyallahu 'anhu), yang termasuk sahabat Nabi ﷺ, bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ mengimami mereka dua rakaat kemudian berdiri tanpa duduk. Orang-orang berdiri bersamanya. Ketika beliau menyelesaikan dua rakaat terakhir, orang-orang menunggu salamnya; beliau lalu bertakbir, sujud, kemudian bertakbir lagi, sujud, lalu salam.

15.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Buhayna al-Asadi (may Allah be pleased with him), the ally of Banu Abdul-Muttalib, that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ stood up in the Dhuhr prayer when he should have sat, and when he completed his prayer, he performed two prostrations while sitting before the taslim, saying the takbir at each prostration. The people prostrated with him to make up for what he had forgotten of the sitting.
15.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Buhaynah al-Asadi (radhiyallahu 'anhu), sekutu Banu Abdul-Muthallib, bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berdiri dalam shalat Dhuhur ketika seharusnya duduk. Ketika beliau menyelesaikan shalatnya, beliau sujud dua kali dalam keadaan duduk sebelum salam, bertakbir pada setiap sujud. Orang-orang sujud bersamanya untuk menggantikan apa yang beliau lupakan dari duduk.

16.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Buhayna al-Azdi (may Allah be pleased with him), the ally of Banu Abdul-Muttalib, that he said: 'The Messenger of Allah ﷺ led us in two rak'ahs then stood up without sitting after the two rak'ahs. The people stood with him. When he completed his prayer, he performed two prostrations while sitting before the taslim, then gave the salam.'
16.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah Ibnu Buhaynah al-Azdi (radhiyallahu 'anhu), sekutu Banu Abdul-Muthallib, bahwa ia berkata: 'Rasulullah ﷺ mengimami kami dua rakaat kemudian berdiri tanpa duduk setelah dua rakaat itu. Orang-orang berdiri bersamanya. Ketika beliau menyelesaikan shalatnya, beliau sujud dua kali dalam keadaan duduk sebelum salam, kemudian salam.'

17.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Buhayna (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ stood in the first two rak'ahs of Dhuhr — or 'Asr — without sitting. When he finished his prayer, he performed two prostrations before giving the taslim.
17.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah Ibnu Buhaynah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berdiri pada dua rakaat pertama shalat Dhuhur — atau Ashar — tanpa duduk. Ketika beliau selesai dari shalatnya, beliau sujud dua kali sebelum salam.

18.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Malik ibn Buhayna (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ went out for Fajr prayer and Ibn al-Qishb was praying. The Prophet ﷺ tapped his shoulder and said: 'O Ibn al-Qishb, are you praying Fajr as four — or twice?' — Ibn Jurayj was uncertain.
18.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah bin Malik Ibnu Buhaynah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ keluar untuk shalat Subuh dan Ibnu al-Qisyb sedang shalat. Nabi ﷺ menepuk bahunya dan berkata: 'Wahai Ibnu al-Qisyb, apakah kamu shalat Subuh empat — atau dua kali?' — Ibnu Juraij ragu-ragu.

19.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that 'Uyaynah ibn Badr, al-Aqra' ibn Habis, and 'Alqamah ibn 'Ulathah gathered at the Prophet ﷺ and mentioned their ancestral lineages. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'If you wish, I can tell you: the ancestor of Banu 'Amir is a red or tawny camel that eats from the edges of trees — and I believe he said: in a meadow — while Ghatafan is a rugged hillock that repels people from it.' Al-Aqra' ibn Habis said: 'Then where is the ancestor of Banu Tamim?' He said: 'Had he stayed silent...'
19.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Uyainah bin Badr, al-Aqra' bin Habis, dan Alqamah bin Ulathah berkumpul di sisi Nabi ﷺ dan menyebut-nyebut kebesaran leluhur mereka. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Jika kalian mau, aku akan memberitahu kalian: leluhur Banu Amir adalah unta merah atau unta cokelat yang makan dari ujung-ujung pohon — dan aku kira beliau berkata: di padang rumput — sedangkan Ghatafan adalah bukit keras yang mengusir orang-orang darinya.' Al-Aqra' bin Habis berkata: 'Lalu di mana leluhur Banu Tamim?' Beliau bersabda: 'Seandainya ia diam...'

20.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was sitting on Hira' and with him were Abu Bakr, 'Umar, and 'Uthman (may Allah be pleased with them all). The mountain trembled, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Be still, O Hira', for upon you there is none but a prophet, a siddiq, or a martyr.'
20.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ sedang duduk di Hira' bersama Abu Bakr, Umar, dan Utsman (radhiyallahu 'anhum). Gunung itu berguncang, maka Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tenang, wahai Hira', karena di atasmu tidak ada kecuali seorang nabi, siddiq, atau syahid.'

21.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "The covenant between us and them is the prayer. Whoever abandons it has disbelieved."'
21.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Perjanjian antara kami dan mereka adalah shalat. Barangsiapa meninggalkannya maka ia telah kafir."'

22.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Truffles are a remedy for the eyes, and 'ajwah dates are from the fruits of Paradise, and this black seed' — Ibn Buraidah said: meaning the nigella that is found in salt — 'is a cure for every disease except death.'
22.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Jamur truffle adalah obat untuk mata, dan kurma ajwah termasuk buah-buahan surga, dan biji hitam ini' — Ibnu Buraidah berkata: yaitu habbatus-sauda yang ada dalam garam — 'adalah obat untuk setiap penyakit kecuali kematian.'

23.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet of Allah ﷺ said: 'Do not say "our master" to a hypocrite, for if he truly is your master, you have angered your Lord, the Almighty.'
23.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi Allah ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah kalian berkata "tuan kami" kepada seorang munafik, karena jika ia memang benar-benar tuan kalian, maka kalian telah membuat murka Rabb kalian Yang Maha Perkasa.'

24.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The people of Paradise will be in one hundred and twenty rows, eighty of them from this ummah.' And 'Affan narrated on one occasion: 'You will be eighty of those rows.'
24.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Penghuni surga ada seratus dua puluh shaf, delapan puluh di antaranya dari umat ini.' Dan Affan meriwayatkan pada satu kesempatan: 'Kalian akan menjadi delapan puluh shaf dari mereka.'

25.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he and his father visited Mu'awiyah, who seated them on cushions. Food was brought and they ate, then a drink was brought. Mu'awiyah drank, then offered it to Buraidah's father. His father said: 'I have not drunk it since the Messenger of Allah ﷺ prohibited it.' Mu'awiyah said: 'I was the most handsome youth of Quraysh and the most pleasant-toothed; there was nothing I enjoyed more — while I was young — except milk, or a person of good conversation.'
25.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia dan ayahnya mengunjungi Muawiyah, yang mendudukkan mereka di atas bantal. Makanan dibawa dan mereka makan, kemudian minuman dibawa. Muawiyah minum lalu menawarkan kepada ayah Buraidah. Ayahnya berkata: 'Aku tidak meminumnya sejak Rasulullah ﷺ mengharamkannya.' Muawiyah berkata: 'Aku dulu pemuda Quraisy yang paling tampan dan paling bagus giginya; tidak ada sesuatu yang lebih aku nikmati — saat aku masih muda — selain susu, atau seseorang yang pandai berbicara.'

26.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I was sitting with the Prophet ﷺ when a man came to him called Maa'iz ibn Malik and said: "O Prophet of Allah, I have committed adultery and I want you to purify me." The Prophet ﷺ told him: "Go back." The next day he came again and confessed adultery before him, and the Prophet ﷺ again told him: "Go back." The Prophet ﷺ then sent to his people and asked about him: "What do you know of Maa'iz ibn Malik al-Aslami? Do you see anything wrong with him, or do you find anything unusual about his mind?" They said: "O Prophet of Allah, we know nothing but good of him..."'
26.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku sedang duduk bersama Nabi ﷺ ketika seorang laki-laki bernama Ma'iz bin Malik datang kepadanya dan berkata: "Ya Nabi Allah, aku telah berzina dan aku ingin engkau menyucikanku." Nabi ﷺ berkata kepadanya: "Pergilah." Keesokan harinya ia datang lagi dan mengaku berzina di hadapan beliau, dan Nabi ﷺ kembali berkata: "Pergilah." Nabi ﷺ kemudian mengutus seseorang kepada kaumnya dan bertanya tentangnya: "Apa yang kalian ketahui tentang Ma'iz bin Malik al-Aslami? Apakah kalian melihat sesuatu yang salah padanya, atau apakah kalian mendapati sesuatu yang aneh pada akalnya?" Mereka berkata: "Ya Nabi Allah, kami tidak mengetahui kecuali kebaikan darinya..."'

27.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he went to visit Mu'awiyah, and there was a man speaking. Buraidah said: 'O Mu'awiyah, give me permission to speak.' Mu'awiyah consented, thinking Buraidah would say something similar to what the other man had said. Buraidah then said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: "I hope to intercede on the Day of Resurrection for as many people as there are trees and clods of earth on the face of the earth."' He then said: 'Do you hope for that, O Mu'awiyah, but not 'Ali ibn Abi Talib (may Allah be pleased with him)?'
27.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia masuk menemui Muawiyah dan ada seorang laki-laki sedang berbicara. Buraidah berkata: 'Wahai Muawiyah, izinkan aku berbicara.' Muawiyah mengizinkan, mengira Buraidah akan mengatakan sesuatu yang serupa dengan apa yang diucapkan laki-laki lain itu. Buraidah kemudian berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Aku berharap dapat memberi syafaat pada Hari Kiamat sebanyak pohon dan gumpalan tanah yang ada di muka bumi."' Kemudian beliau berkata: 'Apakah engkau mengharapkan hal itu, wahai Muawiyah, sedangkan Ali bin Abi Thalib (radhiyallahu 'anhu) tidak mengharapkannya?'

28.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man from Azd died leaving no heir. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Look for an heir for him; look for a relative.' They searched but found none, so the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Give it to the eldest of the Khuza'ah tribe.'
28.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki dari suku Azd meninggal tanpa meninggalkan ahli waris. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Carilah ahli waris untuknya; carilah kerabatnya.' Mereka mencari namun tidak menemukannya, maka Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Serahkanlah kepada yang paling tua dari suku Khuza'ah.'

29.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I participated in a campaign with 'Ali in Yemen and I found some harshness from him. When I came back to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, I mentioned 'Ali and spoke ill of him. I saw the face of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ change, and he said: "O Buraidah, am I not more worthy of the believers than themselves?" I said: "Yes, O Messenger of Allah." He said: "Whoever has me as his master, 'Ali is also his master."'
29.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku pernah berperang bersama Ali di Yaman dan aku mendapati kekasaran darinya. Ketika aku kembali kepada Rasulullah ﷺ, aku menyebut Ali dan menjelek-jelekkannya. Aku melihat wajah Rasulullah ﷺ berubah, dan beliau bersabda: "Wahai Buraidah, bukankah aku lebih berhak atas orang-orang mukmin daripada diri mereka sendiri?" Aku berkata: "Benar, ya Rasulullah." Beliau bersabda: "Barangsiapa yang menjadikan aku sebagai pemimpinnya, maka Ali juga pemimpinnya."'

30.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not take bad omens from anything. But when he intended to visit a woman, he would ask about her name — if it was pleasant, joy could be seen on his face; if it was ugly, that would show on his face. When he sent a man on a mission, he would ask about his name — if his name was good, joy could be seen on his face; if it was unpleasant, that would show on his face.
30.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ tidak mengambil pertanda buruk dari sesuatu apapun. Namun ketika beliau hendak mengunjungi seorang wanita, beliau akan bertanya tentang namanya — jika namanya indah, kegembiraan tampak di wajah beliau; jika jelek, hal itu terlihat di wajah beliau. Ketika beliau mengutus seorang laki-laki dalam suatu misi, beliau akan bertanya tentang namanya — jika namanya bagus, kegembiraan tampak di wajah beliau; jika tidak menyenangkan, hal itu terlihat di wajah beliau.

31.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I heard the Prophet ﷺ say: "I have been sent alongside the Hour, and it nearly came before me."'
31.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Aku diutus bersamaan dengan Hari Kiamat, dan hampir saja ia mendahuluiku."'

32.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ came out to the people one day and called out three times: 'O people, do you know what my example and your example is like?' They said: 'Allah and His Messenger know best.' He said: 'My example and your example is like a people who feared an enemy coming toward them. They sent a man to scout for them, and while he was doing so, he spotted the enemy and came rushing to warn them, fearing the enemy would reach him before he could warn his people. He waved his garment and called out: "O people, the enemy is coming! O people, the enemy is coming!" — three times.'
32.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ keluar menemui orang-orang suatu hari dan menyerukan tiga kali: 'Wahai manusia, tahukah kalian apa perumpamaanku dan perumpamaan kalian?' Mereka berkata: 'Allah dan Rasul-Nya lebih mengetahui.' Beliau bersabda: 'Perumpamaanku dan perumpamaan kalian adalah seperti suatu kaum yang takut akan musuh yang akan datang kepada mereka. Mereka mengutus seorang laki-laki untuk memata-matai bagi mereka. Ketika ia sedang melakukannya, ia melihat musuh lalu berlari untuk memperingatkan mereka, khawatir musuh akan mencapainya sebelum ia bisa memperingatkan kaumnya. Ia melambaikan pakaiannya dan berseru: "Wahai manusia, musuh telah datang! Wahai manusia, musuh telah datang!" — tiga kali.'

33.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I was sitting with the Prophet ﷺ when a woman from Ghamid came and said: "O Prophet of Allah, I have committed adultery and I want you to purify me." The Prophet ﷺ told her: "Go back." The next day she came again and confessed adultery before him and said: "O Messenger of Allah, I have committed adultery and I want you to purify me. Perhaps you will turn me away as you turned away Maa'iz ibn Malik, but by Allah..."'
33.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku sedang duduk bersama Nabi ﷺ ketika seorang wanita dari Ghamid datang dan berkata: "Ya Nabi Allah, aku telah berzina dan aku ingin engkau menyucikanku." Nabi ﷺ berkata kepadanya: "Pergilah." Keesokan harinya ia datang lagi dan mengaku berzina di hadapan beliau dan berkata: "Ya Rasulullah, aku telah berzina dan aku ingin engkau menyucikanku. Mungkin engkau akan menolakku sebagaimana engkau menolak Ma'iz bin Malik, namun demi Allah..."'

34.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I was sitting with the Prophet ﷺ and heard him say: "Learn Surah al-Baqarah, for acquiring it is a blessing and abandoning it is a regret, and the sorcerers cannot bear it." Then after a while he said: "Learn Surah al-Baqarah and Surah Al 'Imran, for they are the two radiant ones that will shade their companion on the Day of Resurrection as if they were two clouds or two canopies or two flocks of birds in formation. And the Quran will meet its companion on the Day of Resurrection, when he rises from his grave, like a pale man saying: "Do you recognize me?" He will say: "I do not recognize you." He will say: "Do you recognize me?" He will say: "I do not recognize you." He will say..."'
34.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku sedang duduk bersama Nabi ﷺ dan mendengar beliau bersabda: "Pelajarilah Surah al-Baqarah, karena mempelajarinya adalah berkah dan meninggalkannya adalah penyesalan, dan para penyihir tidak mampu menanggungnya." Kemudian setelah beberapa saat beliau bersabda: "Pelajarilah Surah al-Baqarah dan Surah Ali Imran, karena keduanya adalah az-zahrawain yang akan menaungi pembacanya pada Hari Kiamat seolah-olah keduanya adalah dua awan atau dua kanopi atau dua kelompok burung yang berjajar. Dan Al-Qur'an akan bertemu pembacanya pada Hari Kiamat, ketika ia bangkit dari kuburnya, seperti seorang laki-laki pucat yang berkata: "Apakah kamu mengenalku?" Ia akan berkata: "Aku tidak mengenalmu." Ia akan berkata: "Apakah kamu mengenalku?" Ia akan berkata: "Aku tidak mengenalmu." Ia akan berkata..."'

35.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I was sitting with the Prophet ﷺ and heard the Prophet ﷺ say: "A people with broad faces and small eyes — as if their faces were shields — will drive my ummah, saying it three times, until they join them to the Arabian Peninsula. As for the first wave, those who flee will be saved; as for the second, some will perish and some will be saved; as for the third, all who remain will be burned." They said: "O Prophet of Allah, who are they?" He said: "They are the Turks." He added: "By the One in Whose hand is my soul, they will surely tie their horses to the pillars of the Muslims' mosques."' And Buraidah would not depart from the Turks.
35.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku sedang duduk bersama Nabi ﷺ dan mendengar Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Suatu kaum dengan wajah lebar dan mata kecil — seolah-olah wajah mereka adalah perisai — akan mengusir umatku, beliau mengucapkannya tiga kali, hingga mereka menyatukan mereka dengan Jazirah Arab. Adapun gelombang pertama, orang-orang yang melarikan diri akan selamat; adapun yang kedua, sebagian akan binasa dan sebagian akan selamat; adapun yang ketiga, semua yang tersisa akan dibakar." Mereka berkata: "Ya Nabi Allah, siapakah mereka?" Beliau bersabda: "Mereka adalah orang-orang Turki." Beliau menambahkan: "Demi Dzat yang jiwaku ada di tangan-Nya, sungguh mereka akan mengikat kuda-kuda mereka ke tiang-tiang masjid kaum muslimin."' Dan Buraidah tidak pernah berpisah dari orang-orang Turki.

36.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that one evening he went out and met the Prophet ﷺ, who took him by the hand and led him into the mosque. There they heard the voice of a man reciting. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'Do you think he is showing off?' Buraidah fell silent. Then there was a man supplicating: 'O Allah, I ask You by my testimony that You are Allah, there is no god but You, the One, the Eternal, Who has not begotten nor been begotten and there is none like unto Him.' The Prophet ﷺ said: 'By the One in Whose hand is Muhammad's soul, he has asked Allah by His Greatest Name — the one by which, when asked, He gives, and when called upon, He answers.' Then the next evening...
36.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa pada suatu malam ia keluar lalu bertemu Nabi ﷺ, yang menggenggam tangannya dan membawanya masuk ke masjid. Di sana mereka mendengar suara seorang laki-laki sedang membaca. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Apakah menurutmu ia sedang riya?' Buraidah diam. Kemudian ada seorang laki-laki berdoa: 'Ya Allah, aku memohon kepada-Mu dengan kesaksianku bahwa Engkau adalah Allah, tidak ada ilah selain Engkau, Yang Maha Esa, Yang Maha Dibutuhkan, Yang tidak beranak dan tidak pula diperanakkan, dan tidak ada seorangpun yang setara dengan-Nya.' Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Demi Dzat yang jiwa Muhammad ada di tangan-Nya, ia telah memohon kepada Allah dengan Nama-Nya yang Paling Agung — yang ketika dimohon dengannya Dia memberi, dan ketika dipanggil dengannya Dia menjawab.' Kemudian pada malam berikutnya...

37.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that his father participated in sixteen military expeditions with the Prophet ﷺ.
37.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah bin Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ayahnya berperang bersama Nabi ﷺ dalam enam belas peperangan.

38.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he participated in sixteen military expeditions with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.
38.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berperang bersama Rasulullah ﷺ dalam enam belas peperangan.

39.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man came to the Prophet ﷺ and asked him about the times of prayer. He said: 'Stay with us for these two days.' He commanded Bilal, when dawn broke, to call the adhan; then commanded him to call the iqamah. He then commanded him to call the adhan for Dhuhr when the sun had passed its zenith; then to call the iqamah. He commanded him to call the iqamah for 'Asr when the sun was still high; then for Maghrib when the rim of the sun had set; then for 'Isha when twilight had disappeared. The next day he commanded him to delay Fajr until it was well lit; then to delay Dhuhr until the heat had cooled considerably; then he prayed 'Asr when the sun was white...
39.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan bertanya tentang waktu-waktu shalat. Beliau bersabda: 'Tinggallah bersama kami selama dua hari ini.' Beliau memerintahkan Bilal, ketika fajar terbit, untuk mengumandangkan adzan; kemudian memerintahkannya untuk mengumandangkan iqamat. Beliau kemudian memerintahkannya untuk mengumandangkan adzan Dhuhur ketika matahari telah tergelincir; kemudian iqamat. Beliau memerintahkannya untuk mengumandangkan iqamat Ashar ketika matahari masih tinggi; kemudian Maghrib ketika tepi matahari telah terbenam; kemudian Isya ketika mega telah menghilang. Keesokan harinya beliau memerintahkannya untuk mengakhirkan Fajar hingga cukup terang; kemudian mengakhirkan Dhuhur hingga panas cukup reda; kemudian shalat Ashar ketika matahari masih putih...

40.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that a woman came to the Prophet ﷺ and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, I gave my mother a slave-girl as charity, but my mother died and the slave-girl came back to me through inheritance.' He said: 'Your reward has been given and she has been returned to you through inheritance.' She said: 'My mother died without having performed Hajj — would it suffice if I perform Hajj on her behalf?' He said: 'Yes.' She said: 'My mother had a month of fasting that she owed — would it suffice if I fast on her behalf?' He said: 'Yes.'
40.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang wanita datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: 'Ya Rasulullah, aku telah menyedekahkan kepada ibuku seorang budak perempuan, tetapi ibuku telah meninggal dan budak perempuan itu kembali kepadaku melalui warisan.' Beliau bersabda: 'Pahalamu telah diberikan dan ia telah dikembalikan kepadamu melalui warisan.' Ia berkata: 'Ibuku meninggal tanpa sempat menunaikan haji — apakah cukup jika aku berhaji atas namanya?' Beliau bersabda: 'Ya.' Ia berkata: 'Ibuku memiliki satu bulan puasa yang belum dibayar — apakah cukup jika aku berpuasa atas namanya?' Beliau bersabda: 'Ya.'

41.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Malihin that he said: 'We were with Buraidah on a military campaign on a cloudy day, and he said: "Perform the prayer early, for the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "Whoever misses the 'Asr prayer, his deeds will be rendered void.""'
41.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Malih bahwa ia berkata: 'Kami bersama Buraidah dalam sebuah perang pada hari yang mendung, dan ia berkata: "Laksanakanlah shalat lebih awal, karena Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Barangsiapa meninggalkan shalat Ashar, maka amalannya akan gugur.""'

42.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I had forbidden you from visiting graves — now visit them. I had forbidden you from keeping the meat of sacrificial animals for more than three days — now keep it for as long as you wish. I had forbidden you from nabidh except in a waterskin — now drink from all types of vessels, but do not drink anything intoxicating.'
42.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku pernah melarang kalian dari ziarah kubur — sekarang ziarahilah. Aku pernah melarang kalian menyimpan daging hewan kurban lebih dari tiga hari — sekarang simpanlah selama yang kalian mau. Aku pernah melarang kalian dari nabidh kecuali dalam tempat air — sekarang minumlah dari semua jenis wadah, tetapi jangan minum yang memabukkan.'

43.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever misses the 'Asr prayer, his deeds will be rendered void.'
43.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa meninggalkan shalat Ashar, maka amalannya akan gugur.'

44.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abdullah ibn Mawala that he said: 'While I was traveling through al-Ahwaz, I came upon a man traveling before me on a mule. He was saying: "O Allah, my generation from this ummah has passed — join me with them." I said: "And me too — include me in your supplication." He said: "And my companion here, if he wishes that." Then he said: "The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: "The best of my ummah is my generation, then those who come after them" — and I do not know whether he mentioned the third or not — "then there will come generations in whom obesity spreads; they will testify without being asked."' And the man turned out to be Buraidah al-Aslami.
44.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abdullah bin Mawala bahwa ia berkata: 'Ketika aku sedang berjalan di al-Ahwaz, aku berjumpa dengan seorang laki-laki yang berjalan di depanku di atas bagal. Ia berkata: "Ya Allah, generasiku dari umat ini telah berlalu — gabungkanlah aku dengan mereka." Aku berkata: "Dan aku juga — sertakan aku dalam doamu." Ia berkata: "Dan temanku ini, jika ia menginginkan hal itu." Kemudian ia berkata: "Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: "Sebaik-baik umatku adalah generasiku, kemudian orang-orang yang mengikuti mereka" — dan aku tidak tahu apakah beliau menyebutkan yang ketiga atau tidak — "kemudian akan datang generasi-generasi yang di dalamnya menyebar kegemukan; mereka bersaksi tanpa diminta."' Dan ternyata orang itu adalah Buraidah al-Aslami.

45.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent them on a military expedition. When they returned, he said: 'How did you find the companionship of your commander?' Buraidah said: 'Either I complained about him, or someone else complained.' He said: 'I raised my head — and I was a man who used to look down — and there was the Prophet ﷺ with his face reddened, saying: "Whoever has me as his guardian, 'Ali is his guardian."'
45.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ mengutus mereka dalam sebuah pasukan. Ketika mereka kembali, beliau bersabda: 'Bagaimana kalian mendapati kebersamaan pemimpin kalian?' Buraidah berkata: 'Entah aku yang mengadukannya atau orang lain yang mengadukannya.' Ia berkata: 'Aku mengangkat kepalaku — dan aku adalah seorang laki-laki yang biasa menunduk — dan di sana ada Nabi ﷺ dengan wajah yang memerah, bersabda: "Barangsiapa yang menjadikan aku sebagai pemimpinnya, maka Ali adalah pemimpinnya."'

46.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'No man gives out any charity until he has freed it from the jaws of seventy devils.'
46.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tidaklah seorang laki-laki mengeluarkan sesuatu dari sedekah hingga ia membebaskannya dari rahang tujuh puluh setan.'

47.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah al-Aslami (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'One day I went out for a need, and there ahead of me was the Prophet ﷺ walking. He took my hand and we walked together. Ahead of us was a man praying with many bowings and prostrations. The Prophet ﷺ said: "Do you think he is showing off?" I said: "Allah and His Messenger know best." He released my hand, then joined his hands together and moved them up and down, saying: "Hold to the middle path! Hold to the middle path! Hold to the middle path! For whoever contends with this religion, it will overcome him."'
47.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah al-Aslami (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Suatu hari aku keluar untuk suatu keperluan, dan di hadapanku ada Nabi ﷺ sedang berjalan. Beliau menggenggam tanganku dan kami berjalan bersama. Di hadapan kami ada seorang laki-laki sedang shalat dengan banyak ruku dan sujud. Nabi ﷺ bersabda: "Apakah menurutmu ia sedang riya?" Aku berkata: "Allah dan Rasul-Nya lebih mengetahui." Beliau melepaskan tanganku, kemudian menyatukan kedua tangannya dan menggerak-gerakannya naik turun, bersabda: "Berpeganglah pada jalan tengah! Berpeganglah pada jalan tengah! Berpeganglah pada jalan tengah! Karena barangsiapa yang memaksakan diri dalam agama ini, maka agama ini akan mengalahkannya."'

48.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'The believer dies with sweat on his forehead.'
48.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Orang mukmin meninggal dengan keringat di dahinya.'

49.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ heard a man saying: 'O Allah, I ask You by my testimony that You are Allah, there is no god but You, the One, the Eternal, Who has not begotten nor been begotten, and there is none like unto Him.' He said: 'He has asked Allah by His Greatest Name — the one by which, when asked, He gives, and when called upon, He answers.'
49.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ mendengar seorang laki-laki berkata: 'Ya Allah, aku memohon kepada-Mu dengan kesaksianku bahwa Engkau adalah Allah, tidak ada ilah selain Engkau, Yang Maha Esa, Yang Maha Dibutuhkan, Yang tidak beranak dan tidak pula diperanakkan, dan tidak ada seorangpun yang setara dengan-Nya.' Beliau bersabda: 'Ia telah memohon kepada Allah dengan Nama Allah yang Paling Agung — yang ketika dimohon dengannya Dia memberi, dan ketika dipanggil dengannya Dia menjawab.'

50.iwh_en: It was narrated from Buraidah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ prayed all the prayers with one wudu on the Day of the Conquest. 'Umar said to him: 'You did something you had not used to do.' He said: 'I did it deliberately.'
50.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Buraidah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ menunaikan semua shalat dengan satu wudhu pada Hari Fathu Makkah. Umar berkata kepadanya: 'Engkau telah melakukan sesuatu yang tidak biasa engkau lakukan.' Beliau bersabda: 'Aku melakukannya dengan sengaja.'
